title: ActionScript Cheat Sheet
description: Quick reference for ActionScript syntax and patterns

commands.description: Declare a variable with a specific type
commands.scenario: Declare a variable with a specific type

commands.command: function add(a:int, b:int):int { return a + b; }
commands.description: Define a function with parameters and return type
commands.scenario: Define a function with parameters and return type

commands.description: Create a simple for loop

commands.command: class Person { public var name:String; public function Person(name:String) { this.name = name; } }
commands.description: Define a class with a constructor
commands.scenario: Define a class with a constructor

commands.description: Create an instance of a class
commands.scenario: Create an instance of a class

commands.command: class Student extends Person { override public function toString():String { return "Student: " + name; } }
commands.description: Extend a class and override a method
commands.scenario: Extend a class and override a method

commands.description: Add an event listener to a button
commands.scenario: Add an event listener to a button

commands.description: Define an event handler function
Remove an event listener

commands.command: try { // code that may throw an error } catch (error:Error) { trace(error.message); }
commands.description: Use try-catch for error handling
Throw a custom error

commands.command: class CustomError extends Error { public function CustomError(message:String) { super(message); } }
commands.description: Define a custom error class

commands.command: class Singleton { private static var instance:Singleton; public static function getInstance():Singleton { if (!instance) { instance = new Singleton(); } return instance; } }
commands.description: Singleton pattern implementation

commands.command: class Subject extends EventDispatcher { public function notify():void { dispatchEvent(new Event("update")); } }
commands.description: Observer pattern using events

commands.command: class ShapeFactory { public function createShape(type:String):Shape { if (type == "circle") return new Circle(); else if (type == "square") return new Square(); return null; } }
commands.description: Factory pattern for object creation
Defines the namespace for a class, essential for organizing large-scale projects and avoiding naming collisions.
Explicitly imports a class from a specific package to make it available in the current scope without using fully qualified names.
Creates a dynamic, heterogeneous array that can store multiple data types simultaneously.
Defines a contract that classes must implement, enabling polymorphism through type checking rather than inheritance.

commands.command: var timer:Timer = new Timer(1000, 5); timer.addEventListener(TimerEvent.TIMER, onTimer); timer.start();
commands.description: Implements scheduled execution of code using the Timer class for periodic or delayed tasks.
